topic_summary: Surface cleaning techniques for aluminum welding: sodium hydroxide, nitric/sulfuric/phosphoric chromic acids, ferrous sulfate. Tom digresses into a reminiscence about chromic acid baths in John Wulff's lab at MIT and the change in environmental regulation since his student days.
key_cases_or_anecdotes: John Wulff's lab chromic acid bath (MIT, Tom's student era)
EPA regulatory contrast: then vs. now
fragment_notes: Transcript is short (~2:46) and begins mid-discussion. Likely a tail end of a class session, ending with Tom shifting to discussion of papers. Not a full 50-minute lecture.
